reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: All three experts converge on weekly subcutaneous injection of retatrutide as a triple-agonist (GLP-1 / GIP / glucagon) and the 2.5 mg weekly dose as a real-world working dose for someone established on the peptide. Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:35:14.680) reads back a member's protocol: "Retatrutide 2.5 mg HRT including testosterone. Lost 30 lbs on Tirz before that" and recommends (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:36:29.320): "increase the Retatrutide. Add in Cagrilintide. Add in Orforglipron if you wanted to." Taylor (G27dIPBJUSs 00:36:29.320) gives the identical "increase the Reta, add in Cagrilintide" answer in her parallel coffee talk. Trevor (igkOl7DpTs0 00:38:16.480): "People find their sweet spot between 2.5 and 4 milligrams a week" and (igkOl7DpTs0 00:38:53.119): "clinical sweet spot 4 to 6 milligrams a week"; Hunter email threadId 1983433d8a4c3655 (2025-07-22) cites the 2025 Lancet trial: "weekly injections of retatrutide (at various doses, with 8 mg being the key 'sweet spot') and were tracked for 36 weeks" producing "a staggering 26.1% of their total fat mass" loss. All three frame retatrutide as triple-agonist: Hunter (1963b87657ac7e4a 2025-04-15): "GLP-1 receptors" "GIP receptors" "Glucagon receptors"; Trevor (6eZTiUoEpcM 00:01:13.119): "GLP1 gip glucagon receptor agonist." All three flag the heart-rate bump (Hunter quantifies it; Trevor ties it to glucagon thermogenesis indirectly via the same physiology).
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Microdosing camp vs. proper-titration camp — sharpest split. Trevor is unequivocal: (PZtKV9DjRxQ 00:00:22.880): "microdosing retatrutide. Are you out of your mind?" and (PZtKV9DjRxQ 00:14:19.280): "You cannot microdose this. You cannot," with the pharmacokinetic explanation (PZtKV9DjRxQ 00:15:08.560): "terminal half-life of about It's approximately 144 hours" and (PZtKV9DjRxQ 00:15:57.120): "It takes four to five half-lives to reach 94 to 97% of steady-state concentration." Taylor takes the OPPOSITE position for off-label vascular indications — (JC1poI1OEog 01:35:07.480): "typically when you see macular degeneration it's a vascular issue to which a microdose of a GLP will help." Both can be true (Trevor is talking about weight-loss / metabolic targets requiring steady-state; Taylor is talking about a microvascular nudge), but the surface message to a patient sounds contradictory and must be disambiguated by goal. Target-dose ceiling: Trevor's masterclass (igkOl7DpTs0 00:38:53.119) says "clinical sweet spot 4 to 6 milligrams a week," while the Lancet trial Hunter cites (1983433d8a4c3655 2025-07-22) used 8 mg as the "sweet spot" — newer trial data (2025) sits HIGHER than Trevor's typical practitioner ceiling. Forever vs. cycle: Trevor (WyUgRv8oRik 00:36:46.640): "It's not a cycle. It is a forever peptide for one simple reason. It restores homeostasis," and (LGO11qnrSb8 00:24:28.559): "when you maintain reatrite at a maintenance dose" indefinitely. Taylor (Cg7JtpnfRY4 00:49:59.600): "I would come off of all GLP's for 4 weeks" before resuming or rotating to Tesofensine — i.e., she's open to defined off-cycles that Trevor explicitly rejects.
